sutta: the buddha said monks these 4 persons are found existing in the world what for the unshaken recluse the blue lotus recluse the white lotus recluse and the recluse who is exquisite among recluses now the first 1 the buddha said how monks is a person an unshaken recluse hearing a man is of right view right thoughts right speech right action right livelihood right effort right mindfulness and right concentration thus a person is an and unshaken recluse and how monks is a person a blue lotus recluse urine among is 1 of right view right thoughts right speech right action right livelihood right effort right mindfulness and right concentration and also uh right knowledge and right release right liberation yet does he not abide experiencing with his own person the 8 deliverances thus a person is a blue lotus recluse and how much is a person a white lotus recluse he similarly has right view all the 8 factors of the iron eightfold partner plus right knowledge and right release and he acted by experiencing with his own person the 8 deliverances is the white lotus recluse and then the recluse exquisite among recluses is as before that means this monk he gets a plentiful supply of ropes etc etc and is also an arahant now when we compare this uttar with the previous 1 you find that the first 1 the unshaken recluse he has the 8 factors of the aryan fold path but he does not have the other 2 right knowledge and right release that means and she can reclose is an arya but not an arahant just like earlier 1 but the blue lotus recluse here you can see here he has all the 10 factors you know the 8 factor of the aryan eightfold path plus right knowledge and right release that means he's an arahant but he does not experience the 8 deliverances also he does not experience the 8 deliverances that means he's the same as the earlier blue lotus recluse but here it is spelled out that he has all the 8 factors of the aryan eightfold path which means that he has got right concentration and right concentration is always defined in the sutas as a 4 janas or 1 pointedness of mind so it is clear from this that the 8 deliverances does not include the 4 janas this is the mistake made by some monks they did not see this suta they only saw the earlier 1 suta 87 and they say that the blue lotus recluse there is an arahant who does not experience the 8 deliverances therefore he does not have the 4 jana but in this utah 18 9 it is clear that the blue lotus recluse has all the 8 factors of the aryan eightfold path plus right knowledge and right release and still he does not experience the 8 deliverances which means that the 8 deliverances has nothing to do with the 4 janus although it has got to do with the arupa janasa it does not have anything to do with the rupa janas so it is very important when we study the sutras to study many sutras and compare them then only you can get the right view if you just take 1 shutdown 2 suitables it's very difficult to understand for example sati patana you cannot just take sati patana the mahasati patana study that suta i think you know all about sati patana because there's a lot of sutras on the sati patana in the sanghita nikaya there's 1 chapter devoted only to sati patana it's called the sati patana samyuta and there if you want to understand about sati patana you have to read that sang utah and so many suitors there and explains sati patana from different angles and then you have a right view so in the same way here is very clear that this blue lotus in the second suta he does not have the 8 deliverances does not experience the 8 deliverances but he has right concentration 1 of the factors of the rna full path which means that he has got the janas the rupa janas so there is nowhere in the sutasana which talks about dry vision arahan never mentioned in the suta a dry vision is something that's only found in the commentaries the later books in the the buddha said very clearly and in 1 of the sutras i had read earlier that sota panda and asaka the first 2 fruition persons they have perfect sila anagami has got perfect sila and samadhi and that means anagami must have the janas and the arahant has got perfect sealah that means all arahants have perfect samadhi which is the jhanas and there's nowhere in the sutasa you can find 1 pointedness of mine now we've come to suta number 4 10.93 the buddha said monks these 4 persons are found existing in the world what for monks a certain person is 1 who gains tranquilization of the mind internally but does not gain the higher wisdom from contemplation of states herein again monks a certain person is 1 who gains the higher wisdom from contemplation of states but does not gain tranquilization of the mind internally here and again monks a certain person is 1 who gains neither of these things yet again a certain person is 1 who gains both these 4 persons are found existing in the world then monks he who gain tranquilization of the mind but not the higher wisdom from contemplation should make an effort to establish the 1 and attain the other then at some future time he is 1 who has gained both of these things then monks he who has gained the higher wisdom from contemplation but not tranquilization of the mind should make an effort to establish the 1 and attain the other then at some future time he is 1 who has gained both then monks he who has gained neither tranquilization of the mind nor the higher wisdom from contemplation should put forth intense desire effort exertion impulse and obstruction mindfulness and attention for the attainment of those profitable states just as monks when 1's turban or head is ablaze for the extinguishing thereof 1 must put forth intense desire effort exertion impulse unobstruction mindfulness and attention even so must that person put forth intense desire and etc etc for the attainment of those profitable states then at some future time he is 1 who has gained both tranquilization of the mind internally and the higher wisdom from contemplation of states so monks he who has gained both these things should make an effort to establish just those profitable states and further to destroy the ah these monks are the 4 persons found existing in the world
commentary: this suta is concerned with meditation and here you find uh there are 2 things that the buddha says should be developed 1 is tranquilization of the mind which is in pali is called asamatta and the other 1 is contemplation of vipasana sometimes people translate vipasana as insight or wisdom but that is not correct because from here you can see and this is 1 of the sutas where it says the higher wisdom from contemplation that means contemplation will lead to the wisdom so vipassana will lead to wisdom or insight so the persona is not inside our wisdom but we personalize contemplation and these are the 2 things that we have to do when we meditate 1 is tranquilization of the of the mind the other 1 is contemplation to give us the insight or the wisdom so there are 2 there are 4 types of persons here the first 1 is a person who has gained uh tranquilization samata but he has not gained the wisdom from contemplation second 1 he has gained the wisdom from contemplation but not tranquilization of the mind and the third 1 is 1 who has neither of these 2 things and the fourth 1 is 1 who has both of these things and from here you you can see here the buddha says that if a person has tranquilization uh he should make an effort to establish the tranquilization to make it stable to make it strong and to attain the other that he has not attained is to attain the wisdom from contemplation and the second case 1 who has the the wisdom from contemplation but not tranquilization he should make an effort to establish his wisdom from contemplation and to attain tranquilization also so in the third case he should put forth an effort to attain both tranquilization as well as contemplation the wisdom from contemplation 1 thing you note here is that the tranquilization is has to be established that means it is not perfect yet and same with contemplation the wisdom from contemplation here is not the perfect wisdom that will make that person an arahant so the buddha says he has to make it uh as to establish it or to attain it um samatha here uh samatha is tranquilization leads to samadhi and vipassana or contemplation leads to panya sometimes people nowadays tend to say that this is such a thing as vipassana but vipassana and it's contemplation of 1 object after another so it is actually contemplation it is not samadhi and it is not the the buddha samadhi because we know now that vipassana or what they call vipassana jana is contemplation of 1 state after another and that is vipasana but samatha is the tranquilization of the mind from contemplating 1 object 1 object and that leads to 1 pointedness of mind samadhi that's why we find for example in the aryan eightfold path you have sati plus samadhi there are 2 different factors they are not 1 factor the seventh factor is uh right mindfulness this is contemplation that is we pass on our practice and that leads to right samadhi and it is very important to note that these 2 are 2 factors it's not 1 factor and it's always separate there's this vipassana or the mindfulness is 1 factor whereas samadhi is the ability to put the mind on 1 thing only and let it stay on that 1 thing so that's why in in a shutter like this it's quite quite clear and that you have to develop both both things the tranquilization as well as the contemplation and we can understand from the last part now you see and that they have to develop both these things and after these 2 are attained and he has to make a further effort to establish these 2 states and use it to destroy the asavas there's 1 suta later on will come across for angutra nikaya

items: a person who gains tranquilization of the mind internally but does not gain the higher wisdom from contemplation
a person who gains the higher wisdom from contemplation but does not gain tranquilization of the mind
a person who gains neither tranquilization of the mind nor the higher wisdom from contemplation
a person who gains both tranquilization of the mind and the higher wisdom from contemplation
